_guardrails: Anchors = Rotorua + Wellington. No documented state care / named welfare program / single statute enabled his rise — do not invent one. Gang material secondary, tightly attributed, never a headline. Migration/dawn-raids = context, not cause. Facts attributed to public accounts of 'My Life, My Fight' (with Madeleine Chapman) and published profiles.

father.value: Sid Adams — Englishman, ex–Royal Navy, settled in New Zealand
father.source: Published profiles; accounts of 'My Life, My Fight'

mother.value: Lilika Ngauamo — Tongan, worked as a nurse
mother.source: Published profiles; accounts of 'My Life, My Fight'

family_size.value: An exceptionally large family; the exact number of children is reported imprecisely — Adams notes even the family disputed it
family_size.source: Accounts of 'My Life, My Fight' (with M. Chapman)
family_size.note: Humanising beat — use the ambiguity, do not fix a number.

half_sister.value: Dame Valerie Adams — two-time Olympic shot put champion

father_death.value: Died of stomach cancer, 2007 — Steven was 13; he began disengaging from school

drift.value: Brief adolescent drift after his father's death
drift.note: SECONDARY. Never a headline or pull quote. Not sensationalised.

intervention_cameron.value: Blossom Cameron took responsibility for him and moved him from Rotorua to Wellington
intervention_cameron.source: Published profiles; accounts of 'My Life, My Fight'

brother_warren.value: Brother Warren — a former NZ national-team player — contacted Kenny McFadden
brother_warren.note: Let the autobiography arbitrate Cameron/Warren sequence.

mcfadden.value: Kenny McFadden — American ex–Washington State guard who made Wellington home; ran 6 a.m. and afternoon sessions for years; his rule: school every day if you want to play

mcfadden_arranged_scholarship.value: McFadden arranged the scholarship to Scots College, Wellington (Presbyterian, independent private school)

adams_quote_scots.value: "What happened to me at Scots is they lit the spark for me."

ncaa_clearance.value: Concern over NCAA eligibility; he graduated and cleared the process

notre_dame_prep.value: Notre Dame Preparatory School, Fitchburg, Massachusetts — transitional stretch before Pittsburgh

pitt.value: One season at Pittsburgh: started all 32 games, 7.2 pts / 6.3 reb / 2.0 blk, Big East All-Rookie Team

draft.value: Selected 12th overall, 2013 NBA Draft (Oklahoma City Thunder)

adams_funded_scholarship.value: In 2014, within a year of reaching the NBA, Adams funded a scholarship at Scots College himself — reported up to ~NZ$240,000 — for a basketball player showing "determination, humility, and commitment", with McFadden helping select the recipient
adams_funded_scholarship.note: Verify current status on Scots' site (PENDING).

scots_scholarship_current_status.fetch: Scots College website — current scholarship page (does the Adams scholarship still run?) + Wayback for 2007–2011 fees

rotorua_suburb.fetch: 'My Life, My Fight' — exact suburb/street in Rotorua

prepublish_task: Verify all biography stations against 'Steven Adams: My Life, My Fight' (with Madeleine Chapman) + at least one long-form profile before publish.
